# Showcase Dashboard

<!-- GENERATED by scripts/update-showcase.py; do not edit by hand. -->

Source snapshot: `2026-05-18`.
Repository reference: source-controlled generated snapshot.

This page is the GitHub-facing status board for pdocker-android. It is generated
from repository-owned documents so the public story follows the implementation
instead of drifting into a separate marketing copy.

## Product Signal

pdocker-android is a Docker-compatible Android workbench packaged as a native
APK. It combines `pdockerd`, Compose/Dockerfile controls, image/container file
browsing, persistent logs, editor tabs, and `-it`-style terminals inside the
app UI. The current research front is real Android direct execution plus
Vulkan/OpenCL GPU bridging for llama.cpp-class workloads.

## Timeline

The live planning page is generated from [`docs/plan/TODO.md`](../plan/TODO.md)
with a small buffer over the recent implementation pace. Device builds, GPU
bridge work, and long Compose templates are expected to move slower than static
UI/docs work, so the public timeline is intentionally a little conservative.

- [Roadmap timeline](ROADMAP_TIMELINE.md)

## llama.cpp GPU Pulse

- Latest artifact: `2026-05-17T10:32:55Z`
- GPU entry: standard Vulkan loader through pdocker-vulkan-icd.so
- Target met: `false`; speedup: `0.0`
- Current blocker: Android Vulkan rejected a ggml generic SPIR-V compute pipeline with VK_ERROR_FEATURE_NOT_PRESENT
- llama.cpp modified: `false`

## Demo Route

1. Start the APK and show the Overview cards rather than a host shell.
2. Run Compose up from the UI and keep the build log visible.
3. Open the container card, service URL, logs, files, and `-it` terminal.
4. Show the VS Code Server and llama.cpp templates as real project-library
   entries.
5. Finish with storage metrics, compatibility docs, and the current GPU
   blocker so viewers can tell what is working and what is research.
